acceptance: A process/runtime-owned libcurl multi handle is initialized, driven, and cleaned up safely.
notes: Use multi-socket integration rather than blocking easy perform.

acceptance: A single ordinary URL transfer can be represented as a curl easy handle with callbacks mapped to aria2-next state.

scope: Data callback and piece write boundary
acceptance: Received bytes write through existing storage safely, with offset and length truth preserved.
notes: Storage redesign is later CM-014; this checkpoint should minimize storage churn.

acceptance: curl errors, HTTP status failures, retryable failures, and completion events map to existing result semantics.
notes: Do not create a parallel error universe.

acceptance: A simple ranged HTTP/HTTPS download can complete through the curl path without native HTTP client ownership.
notes: Temporary smoke artifacts stay outside the repository.
